notable_pick_trades: Traded 2026 1st (No. 20) and 2027 1st to Dallas for Micah Parsons. Received 2027 4th from Dallas for Rashan Gary.
roster_context: Super Bowl-or-bust window with Jordan Love and Micah Parsons (ACL, likely misses early 2026 games) as cornerstones. Significant 2025 free agent departures (Walker, Doubs, Willis, Quay Walker, Gary, Enagbare) create short-term holes on an otherwise talented roster. New DC Jonathan Gannon installing a multiple 3-4 base scheme with heavy quarters coverage and three-safety looks. No first-round pick limits ability to address needs with premium talent; must be surgical with pick No. 52 overall.

needs.explanation: Rashan Gary was traded to Dallas and Kingsley Enagbare signed with the Jets, gutting the edge rusher room. Micah Parsons (ACL) could miss the first 4-6 games. The remaining options are Lukas Van Ness, Barryn Sorrell, Collin Oliver, and Brenton Cox Jr. (re-signed on a cheap deal after missing most of 2025 with a groin injury). Green Bay needs at least one more starting-caliber edge to bridge the gap until Parsons returns and provide insurance.
needs.current_starter: Micah Parsons (ACL recovery), Lukas Van Ness
needs.depth: Barryn Sorrell, Collin Oliver, Brenton Cox Jr. — young and unproven as primary starters
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2 (No. 52) — could be the pick if a top EDGE falls

needs.explanation: Nate Hobbs was released after a disastrous one-year, $48M experiment. Benjamin St-Juste was signed (2yr/$10M) as a low-cost addition but is unproven as a starter. Keisean Nixon is the only established boundary corner. Carrington Valentine had inconsistent stretches in 2025. Javon Bullard excels in the slot but boundary CB remains a major weakness. Both Nixon and Valentine are free agents after 2026, making this a need for both now and the future.
needs.current_starter: Keisean Nixon, Carrington Valentine / Benjamin St-Juste
needs.depth: Javon Bullard (slot/safety hybrid), Kamal Hadden (returning from injury), Bo Melton (CB experiment TBD)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3 — deep CB draft class favors finding value on Day 2

needs.explanation: Kenny Clark was traded as part of the Parsons deal, and Colby Wooden was dealt to Indianapolis for Zaire Franklin. Javon Hargrave was signed (2yr/$23M) to reunite with Gannon but is 33 years old — a short-term fix. Devonte Wyatt is returning from a fractured tibia/torn ankle ligaments. The nose tackle spot is desperately thin with only Nazir Stackhouse and the injured Jordon Riley (torn Achilles). Jonathan Ford was re-signed but is a depth piece. Gannon's scheme requires three 300-pound linemen up front to stop the run.
needs.current_starter: Devonte Wyatt (returning from injury), Javon Hargrave
needs.depth: Karl Brooks, Warren Brinson, Nazir Stackhouse, Jonathan Ford, Jordon Riley (PUP list candidate)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3 — strong DT draft class aligns well with need

needs.explanation: Rasheed Walker signed with Carolina after starting 52 of 55 games over three years. Jordan Morgan (2024 1st-round pick) is expected to take over at LT but has never started there full-time in the NFL — he bounced between four OL positions over two years. His arm length (32 7/8 inches, 9th percentile) is a concern. Zach Tom is the RT anchor but is recovering from a partially torn patellar tendon suffered in Week 15. Swing tackle depth is limited to Darian Kinnard. If Morgan struggles, the Packers have no fallback plan.
needs.current_starter: Jordan Morgan (LT, unproven), Zach Tom (RT, returning from knee surgery)
needs.depth: Darian Kinnard (swing tackle), Donovan Jennings — very thin beyond starters
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3-5 — depth piece and insurance for Morgan

needs.explanation: Sean Rhyan was re-signed (3yr/$33M) to start at center after a solid late-season audition. Aaron Banks remains at LG but has underperformed his $18M/yr contract. Anthony Belton is a sophomore 2nd-round pick at RG who ranked 68th of 71 qualifying guards in PFF grade as a rookie. Elgton Jenkins was released. The starters are locked in for 2026 but both guard spots are below average. Depth is a concern, though the line is under contract through at least 2028-29.
needs.current_starter: Aaron Banks (LG), Sean Rhyan (C), Anthony Belton (RG)
needs.depth: Lecitus Smith, Jacob Monk — minimal proven depth

needs.explanation: Josh Jacobs is the bell cow and under contract through 2027 (with a Pro Bowl 2025 season: 929 yards, 13 TDs). But Emanuel Wilson signed with Seattle, leaving the backup spot barren. Chris Brooks was re-signed but is a special-teams ace and blocking back, not a viable RB2 ball-carrier. MarShawn Lloyd has played one NFL game since being drafted in 2024 and has zero games in 2025 due to injury. Green Bay needs a reliable backup/change-of-pace back.
needs.depth: Chris Brooks (ST/blocker), MarShawn Lloyd (perpetually injured) — no reliable RB2

needs.explanation: Tucker Kraft is a strong starter (returning from offseason injury but expected to be healthy). Luke Musgrave has not developed as hoped and is entering the final year of his rookie deal. Josh Whyle was re-signed for depth. The Packers lack a true blocking tight end. Drafting a TE for the future could make sense given Musgrave's uncertain 2027 outlook.
needs.depth: Luke Musgrave, Josh Whyle — Musgrave has disappointed, Whyle is a depth piece

needs.explanation: Romeo Doubs signed with the Patriots (4yr/$70M), but the Packers still have a strong WR room with Christian Watson, Jayden Reed, Dontayvion Wicks, and Matthew Golden. Watson and Reed are ascending talents. Skyy Moore was signed as a returner/depth piece. Five of seven rosterable WRs are in contract years, which could create future issues, but the 2026 starting corps is solid. Not a draft priority unless an exceptional BPA opportunity arises.
needs.depth: Dontayvion Wicks, Matthew Golden, Bo Melton, Savion Williams (IR), Skyy Moore

free_agency_impact: Traded Rashan Gary to Cowboys for 2027 4th-round pick — EDGE is now critical need
Lost Kingsley Enagbare (UFA) to Jets (1yr/$10M) — further depletes EDGE depth
Lost Rasheed Walker (UFA) to Panthers (1yr/$10M) — creates LT vacancy, Morgan steps in
Lost Romeo Doubs (UFA) to Patriots (4yr/$70M) — reduces WR depth but room still strong
Lost Quay Walker (UFA) to Raiders (3yr/$40.5M) — replaced via Zaire Franklin trade
Lost Malik Willis (UFA) to Dolphins — backup QB now Desmond Ridder/Kyle McCord
Lost Emanuel Wilson (UFA) to Seahawks — RB2 spot now vacant
Released Elgton Jenkins — saved $19.5M cap space, Rhyan locked in at C
Released Nate Hobbs (post-June 1 cut) — $48M bust after one season, saves $8.8M
Signed DT Javon Hargrave (2yr/$23M) — addresses DT partially, reunites with Gannon
Signed CB Benjamin St-Juste (2yr/$10M) — adds CB depth/competition, not a surefire starter
Re-signed C Sean Rhyan (3yr/$33M) — locks in center position
Re-signed EDGE Brenton Cox Jr. (1yr/$2.5M) — minimal EDGE depth addition
Re-signed RB Chris Brooks (2yr/$4.9M) — special teams contributor, not RB2 answer
Re-signed OT Darian Kinnard (RFA) — swing tackle depth
Re-signed DT Jonathan Ford (1yr) — DL depth piece
Traded DT Colby Wooden to Colts for LB Zaire Franklin — swapped DL depth for LB starter
Signed WR/KR Skyy Moore (1yr) — return specialist, not offensive contributor
Hired Jonathan Gannon as DC — scheme change from 4-3 to multiple 3-4, changes personnel needs
scheme_fit_notes: New DC Jonathan Gannon is installing a multiple 3-4 base defense with heavy use of quarters (Cover 4) coverage and three-safety looks. This scheme requires three 300-pound DL up front (not undersized 3-techs), making nose tackle a premium need. Gannon's scheme generates pressure from the front four rather than blitzing, increasing the value of interior pass-rush ability. His three-safety packages (McKinney, Evan Williams, Javon Bullard) can replace a traditional LB, reducing the urgency at off-ball LB. Boundary CB quality matters because Gannon's quarters coverage puts corners in one-on-one matchups against WR1s. EDGE prospects should be versatile enough to stand up in a 3-4 set. Gannon previously reunited with Hargrave and thrived with pass-rush DTs like Fletcher Cox in Philly.
